DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The allegations in the complaint combined with judicially noticeable documents are: (1) Plaintiffs sought to refinance their existing loan in February 2019 (Complaint, paragraph 9); (2) Plaintiffs were approached by defendant on March 25, 2019 regarding a re-finance offer (Complaint, paragraph 11); (3) Plaintiffs executed the Deed of Trust securing a Note on March 28, 2019 and the Deed of Trust was recorded on March 29, 2019 (Deed of Trust, Assignment of Leases and Rents, Security Agreement, and Fixture Filing); (3) at some point between March 25, 2019 (when Plaintiff where approached by Defendants) and March 28, 2019 (when Plaintiff obtained the operative loan), Plaintiffs "passed on opportunities with other lenders" (Complaint, paragraph 11, 12); (5) on April 1, 2019 Plaintiffs were informed of the favorable terms of the loan (Complaint, paragraph 11); (5) on an unspecified date in April 2019 Plaintiffs were informed that the terms of the offered loan have changed to less favorable (Complaint, paragraph 14); (6) Plaintiffs told Eddie Salem that these loan terms were not what he proposed before, but he was unwilling to do anything to change the final loan terms. Plaintiffs had to take the loan because their existing loan was coming due and they were out of time at that point. (Complaint, paragraph 16).
In opposition to the demurrer (no courtesy copy received in violation of SFRL 2.7B) Plaintiff fails to address the inconsistent timeline: recorded document dated March 28, 2019 vs. the purported date of the execution of the documents in "April 2019." Plaintiffs fails to allege any facts or explain in opposition how Plaintiffs were damaged by or relied on the representation made in April 2019 regarding their loan obtained in March 2019. Plaintiffs were provided multiple opportunities to amend their complaint. They did not do so.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
